entry.title: SAP Cloud ERP (S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) 2602亮点前瞻：跨技术话题（Cross Technology Topic）
entry.content:
又到了半年一度版本升级的日子。在这篇文章里，我会介绍一些在2602版本中跨技术话题中值得分享的一些亮点：

 

目录

一、数据迁移

  1. 新应用：Explore Migration Objects
  2. 2602版本中迁移对象的变化

二、用户体验

  1. /n /o重出江湖
  2. 下载和上载空间
  3. Launchpad的新布局

三、其他亮点

  1. 视图浏览器：图形化展示CDS之间的关系
  2. 权限的更大灵活性：直接给业务角色分配IAM应用

 

 

一、数据迁移

1. 新应用：Explore Migration Objects

         在Cloud ERP数据迁移领域，现在已经有超过200个迁移对象。虽然我们能够在每个迁移对象的说明文档中查到该迁移对象是否有前序依赖的对象，但在如此多的迁移对象的情况下，我们一直缺少一个可视化的界面，能够让我们在项目实施过程中更快速地看到迁移对象之间的依赖关系。

         现在我们推出了一个全新的应用：Explore Migration Objects。通过该应用，我们通过图形化的方式显示某个特定的迁移对象有哪些前序依赖的迁移对象。如下图所示，如果我们想要导入“产品-按新组织级别扩展现有记录”，它的必要的前序对象会用橙色的实线显示，我们必须先导入迁移对象“产品”。它的可选的前序对象会用灰色的虚线显示，“利润中心”和“生产供应区”是它的两个非必要的前序对象。

         这个应用可以帮助我们在项目阶段更方便和准确地确定迁移范围。同时，在图形界面上，你也可以点击依赖的迁移对象，扩展显示与它相关的迁移对象。图形界面可以随意扩展。

 

除了按照迁移对象查找，这个应用也支持按照范围项目（Scope Item）查找。可以查找某个范围项目下有哪些相关的迁移对象。比如下图举例，我想查找范围项目BMD（采购合同）牵涉到哪些迁移对象，我也可以找出这个信息，用图形化的方式显示：

 

 

最后，说明一下体验该应用需要分配业务目录Data Migration – Cockpit（SAP_CA_BC_MIG_PC）。

 

2. 2602版本中迁移对象的变化

关于2602版本中迁移对象的变化，比如新增的对象、更改的对象、删除的对象等，该信息已经发布在SAP Help Portal，具体可通过以下链接查看：

版本 2602 的更改概览 | SAP Help Portal

 

二、用户体验

1. /n /o重出江湖

         几乎所有使用过SAP传统GUI界面的用户都非常熟悉/n和/o这两个指令，/n+tcode就能在当前界面快速打开新的应用。当我需要保留当前界面的同时打开新界面时，输入/o+tcode就能在新界面中打开应用。每一个SAP用户都会熟练操作/n和/o的指令。如今，从2602版本开始，这两个指令又回来了。

         只不过Fiori应用不再是以前的tcode，取而代之的是Fiori ID。比如应用“IAM信息系统”的Fiori ID是F2450。我可以在任何界面的右上角查找框里输入“/nf2450”，就会在当前网页打开应用“IAM信息系统”：

 

 

输入/nf2450后，按回车键：

 

 

同样，上面的这个例子，如果我输入的是/of2450，然后回车，会在一个新的页面打开“IAM信息系统”这个应用。

 

另外，如果你只输入/n，然后回车，会快速回到主页。

如果你只输入/o，然后回车，会在一个新的页面打开主页的界面。

这些操作也是和以前SAP GUI的习惯保持一致的。

 

2. 下载和上载空间

         从这个版本开始，我们可以批量下载和上载空间（Space）了。下载和上载操作也会包含所选空间下面的页面（Page）。这个功能，对于跨系统间的操作，系统之间不能传输的，可以快速地复用我们所设计的空间。

         在“管理快速启动板空间”App上，之前的界面是这样的：

 

这个版本升级之后，会新增“下载”和“上载”两个操作链接：

 

操作的基本流程如下：

  1. 在源系统中，选中一至多个想要下载的空间，点击“下载”。
  2. 会下载一个JSON文件，文件名为Spaces_系统名_日期时间.json。
  3. 打开目标系统，打开管理快速启动板空间。
  4. 点击“上载”，选中之前下载的JSON文件，然后上传。

 

3. Launchpad的新布局

         在上个版本中，我们推出了Launchpad的新布局：增强的导航菜单，在SAP徽标旁边显示产品名称。同时，用户菜单也做了显示优化和顺序调整。

         新界面下左上角的导航菜单：

 

新界面下右上角的用户菜单：

 

从这个版本开始，新布局将成为默认现实。因此，强烈建议在“激活功能”中，激活下面的选项以开启Launchpad的新布局：

/UI2/SHELL_BAR_TOGGLE

 

三、其他亮点

1. 视图浏览器：图形化展示CDS之间的关系

         应用“视图浏览器”（View Browser）一直是我们客户最常用的应用之一，所有与CDS视图相关的操作如自定义接口、报表等操作都绕不开使用这个应用。在2602版本中，视图浏览器首次新增了图形化展示CDS视图依赖关系的功能。图形的方式能更直观、更方便地给我们的客户展示CDS视图（包括表）之间的关系，相信这个功能能提升大家对这个应用的使用体验。

         例如，在视图浏览器中，当我输入I_SalesOrder并选中，页签中增加了“相关性图表”这一项：

 

然后，在主界面中，你会看到与I_SalesOrder有直接关系的其他CDS视图以及相关的表：

 

比如，绿色的代表已经发布的CDS视图，灰色的代表尚未发布的CDS视图，蓝色的代表数据库表。对于CDS视图，你还能看到关键用户扩展和云开发扩展各自的发布状态。

你还可以点击右上角的“转置”链接，图形界面可以从垂直展示的方式变成水平展示，如下所示：

 

 

2. 权限的更大灵活性：直接给业务角色分配IAM应用

         在之前的版本中，我们推出了IAM应用程序这个概念，在权限设计上以应用而不是业务目录作为最小单元，给了权限分配极大的灵活性。但是，我们还是需要首先给业务角色分配相应的业务目录，然后在已分配的权限里做调整。

         从这个版本开始，我们甚至可以直接给业务角色分配IAM应用程序，可以不需要分配业务目录。简单来说，之前如果需要分配某个应用的权限，你需要首先找到这个应用相关的业务目录并分配，然后再做调整，把不需要的应用去除。现在，你可以直接给你的业务角色分配想要的应用访问权限。下面，我将通过一个例子来做解释：

         假设我设计了一个数据迁移的业务角色MIGRATION_USER，我给它分配了一个数据迁移的业务目录，你能看到它能访问数据迁移工具相关的应用，如下所示：

 

在IAM应用程序页签下，点击右上角的编辑按钮，你可以看到有一个“添加”按钮：

 

         假设我需要我的这个角色的用户在导入数据之后，可以查看销售订单和采购订单的数据，以验证导入的数据是否正确。我可以点击添加按钮，查找相关的IAM应用并添加如下：

 

         你能看到，我直接添加了“显示销售订单”和“显示采购订单”的权限，分配类型为“直接”，也就是我直接添加的权限，而不是通过业务目录分配而得。有了这种方式，对于我们需要配置非常个性化的业务角色将会更加方便。

 

         以上是关于SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602版本跨技术话题的一些重要分享，希望对你的日常使用有所帮助。如果你有任何问题，欢迎留言或者私信联系！

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.   

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

In this blog, I’m thrilled to guide you through the latest AI innovations in our SAP Cloud ERP release 2602. This release marks a significant leap forward in our journey to embed reliable, relevant, and responsible AI directly into the core of your ERP. Our goal is simple: to make your work smarter, your decisions faster, and your daily tasks more intuitive.

With our AI co-pilot Joule and a host of new embedded AI capabilities, we're transforming the user experience and delivering tangible business value across every line of business. Let's dive into the highlights!

A Smarter Foundation: Cross-Topic AI Innovations

We're embedding AI into the very fabric of the user experience, making the system more proactive, helpful, and personalized for every user.

1. Situation handling

1.1. AI-assisted situation handling

Solve business challenges faster and smarter with AI-powered recommendations:

  • Instant, Personalized Guidance: Get tailored advice right when you need it, helping you resolve business issues quickly and confidently.
  • Flexible and Customizable: Adapt the solution to your unique business logic—make changes easily and keep your processes up to date.
  • Effortless Access to Knowledge: Find the information you need, even beyond SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, with simplified, contextual support.

Key Features:

  • Generate actionable recommendations based on your specific business context.
  • Create and manage prompts to ensure solutions are relevant and effective.
  • Enrich recommendations with data from multiple sources for deeper insights.

With AI-Assisted Situation Handling, you empower your team to work smarter, make better decisions, and stay agile in a fast-changing business environment.

For more information, see: Situation Handling

Disclaimer: The feature described above is planned for a future release and is not yet generally available. A dedicated blog post will be published once it becomes available

 

2. User experience

2.1. AI-assisted error explanation for SAP Fiori elements-based Applications

With AI-assisted error explanations for SAP Fiori elements-based applications, you’ll save valuable time, boost reliability, and make maintenance easier than ever. Faster resolution of errors with the support of generative AI:

  • Speed Up Maintenance: Let generative AI quickly explain errors and recommend solutions, so you spend less time fixing issues and more time driving your business forward.
  • Reduce Future Errors: Understand the root causes behind problems, helping you prevent similar issues from happening again.

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

2.2. AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home for applications

Tailor My Home experience using natural language and transform your workspace with just a few words:

  • Natural Language Personalization: Simply tell SAP what you need—AI understands your requests and instantly suggests the most relevant apps for your daily tasks.
  • Quick and Easy Setup: Add new applications to your “My Home” in seconds. Preview AI-generated suggestions before confirming, so you always get what fits best.
  • Save Time, Boost Productivity: No more searching through menus. AI streamlines your workflow, making it ideal for both new and occasional users.
  • Trustworthy Recommendations: Rely on intelligent, data-driven suggestions that ensure accuracy and build confidence in your setup.
  • Seamless Experience: Enjoy a smooth, click-minimizing process with a preview-based confirmation—making personalization simple and enjoyable.

Unlock the full potential of SAP Cloud ERP by letting AI tailor your “My Home” to your needs—so you can focus on what matters most

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

2.3. AI-assisted easy fill of SAP Fiori elements-based applications

Let AI handle the details—just describe what you need, and watch your forms fill themselves:

  • Save Time Instantly: Maintain all the fields on your object pages in seconds, thanks to seamless AI-powered automation.
  • No More Guesswork: Simply use natural language, and the system intelligently matches your words to the right fields—no more hunting through forms.

With AI-assisted easy fill, you’ll breeze through data entry, reduce manual effort, and focus on what matters most in your business.

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

2.4. AI-assisted task automation (beta)

AI-assisted task automation is the next embedded AI feature in the SAP Fiori user experience of SAP Cloud ERP. It builds on the previously delivered features such as easy filter, easy fill and smart solutions for situations in My Home.​

It allows key users and end users to automate their daily activities in SAP’s Fiori apps by using generative AI to create so called “smart helpers”. Those ”smart helpers” execute the repetitive activities on behalf of the end user either from the SAP Fiori launchpad or from individual SAP Fiori apps while always keeping the user in the loop for decisions on data changes.​

Benefits:

  • Easy to use SAP Fiori application to create “smart helpers”​
  • Full control of what the “smart helper” proposes to do​
  • Intuitive way of running the “smart helper” while being always in control in case of changes​
  • Supports all SAP Fiori elements-based applications​

Key features:

  • Creation of “smart helpers” based on all available SAP Fiori elements-based applications in SAP Cloud ERP​
  • Generation and validation of execution plan for “smart helpers”​
  • Run time for “smart helpers” is integrated into My Home the product homepage of SAP Cloud ERP​

Please register to be part of the SAP Beta Testing of the Newest AI Innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

2.5. AI-assisted smart solution for situations (beta)

AI-assisted smart solution for situations in My Home simplifies workflows by dynamically adapting the user interface to guide business users through resolution steps using the power of generative AI

Directly on the SAP Cloud ERP homepage, users can resolve operational situations in just a few clicks. AI-driven recommendations accelerate decision-making and streamline user actions for improved efficiency

Benefits:

  • Drives time efficiency by eliminating navigation through intelligent UI design
  • Enhances decision-making with timely, context-aware AI recommendations
  • Delivers trustworthy AI-generated results, built on enterprise-grade intelligence
  • Offers an intuitive, click-efficient experience, empowering users to act confidently with minimal effort.

Key features:

  • Key information and actions are surfaced through dynamic cards reducing the need for in-depth navigation
  • Enables one-click resolution of situations directly from the homepage thereby enhancing operational efficiency within SAP Cloud ERP
  • Situation types supported:
  • End date for project reached but stage not completed
  • Alert for bank messages
  • Expiring Sales Quotations

Please register to be part of the SAP Beta Testing of the Newest AI Innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

AI Innovations Across Lines of Business:

3. Finance

3.1. Accounting Accruals agent (beta)

Close Your Books Faster with the AI-Powered Accounting Accruals Agent

The agent helps accountants to automate journal entry preparation for accruals by analyzing historical data and leveraging accounting policy documents. It calculates accurate amounts and provides a pre-populated list of journal entries for review and confirmation. Your accounts will increase their productivity, ensure timely month-end close, and get more time for high-value activities.

  • Automate Journal Entries: Let AI handle the heavy lifting by preparing accrual journal entries for you—twice as fast as before.
  • Boost Accuracy: The agent analyzes your historical data and accounting policies to calculate precise accrual amounts, reducing errors and manual work.
  • Ready-to-Review Entries: Receive a pre-populated list of journal entries, so your team can quickly review and confirm, saving valuable time.
  • Focus on What Matters: Free up your accountants to concentrate on high-value activities, ensuring a timely and efficient month-end close.

Business Impact:

  • Cut time spent on accrual calculations and deferrals by up to 80%
  • Reduce review and posting time by 50%
  • Achieve a faster, smoother period-end close

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

3.2. International trade classification agent (beta)

Classify your products for international shipping in seconds—no expertise required

This agent helps product classification specialists to classify goods for shipping. The agent reasons over product characteristics and trade regulations and acts to classify goods for international shipping. The agent recommends customs tariff numbers and commodity codes, providing rationale for easy review and approval.

This agent will help ensure compliance with global trade regulations and reduce the risk of manual errors with robust governance and a clear, audit-ready decision

  • Automatic Classification: Let AI analyze your product details and trade regulations to instantly recommend the right customs tariff numbers and commodity codes.
  • Ensure Compliance: Rely on recommendations that are reasoned from both product characteristics and up-to-date trade rules, helping you meet global regulations with confidence.
  • Reduce Risk: Eliminate manual errors with a robust, auditable decision process—every classification comes with clear rationale for easy review and approval.

Business Impact:

  • 50% reduction in effort to classify international trade products

For more information, see: Discovery Center

 

3.3. Sales accounting: period comparisons and business insights for Product Profitability Review Booklet

The Product Profitability Review Booklet enables you, as a Sales Accountant, to analyze the profitability for products sold using built-in business pages for the Margin Overview and the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Breakdown.

Benefits:

  • Gain deeper insights into margins and cost of goods sold (COGS) with period comparisons
  • Enable informed decision-making with AI-enabled financial business insights
  • Support flexible market segment analysis with an extensible CDS view
  • Improve visibility with additional currency types and dimensions

Key features:

Enhancements to the Product Profitability Review Booklet app that:

  • Provide new period comparisons for product profitability
  • Use AI to generate financial business insights into period comparisons for product profitability
  • Add a commenting feature
  • Display results in functional currency
  • Enable CDS view to support extensibility for market segment
  • Incorporate additional dimensions and navigation targets

For more information, see: Enhancements in the Product Profitability Review Booklet.

 

3.4. SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity: Using Joule to access solution-related content 

Have a question about SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity? Whether you’re curious about features, setup steps, or integration scenarios, Joule is here to help! With Joule, you can simply ask your question in natural language—no technical jargon needed. Joule instantly searches the SAP Help Portal, analyzes the latest resources, and delivers clear, context-aware answers tailored to your needs.

Benefits:

  • Ask Naturally, Get Answers Instantly: Express your business needs in your own words and receive quick, relevant guidance.
  • Faster Self-Service: Get crisp, summarized content so you can resolve issues or learn new features without digging through documentation.
  • Save Time: Joule streamlines your search, helping you find the right information faster so you can focus on your work.

Key features:

  • Smart Search & Summaries: Joule scans SAP Help Portal documentation and summarizes the most important details about SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity processes and features.
  • Easy Deep Dives: Need more detail? Joule provides direct links to the original documentation for further exploration.

For more information, see: Joule with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity | SAP Help Portal

 

3.5. SAP Green Ledger: Joule for reporting: AI-assisted carbon emission analysis (09/2025)

Instantly Visualize and Understand Your Carbon Data with Joule in SAP Green Ledger

Joule in Green Ledger can generate charts from carbon data through conversational prompts, providing automatic visualizations for reporting purposes. This tool is useful for efficiently analyzing and presenting carbon data in a clear and intuitive format.

Benefits:

  • Actionable Insights for Sustainability: Instantly see trends and breakdowns in your greenhouse gas emissions, empowering you to make smarter decisions for carbon reduction and long-term success.
  • Save Time, Focus on Strategy: Automated reporting means less manual work and more time for impactful analysis and decision-making.
  • Effortless for Everyone: The intuitive interface makes advanced carbon reporting accessible to all users, regardless of technical background.

Key features:

  • Tailored Visualizations: Generate column, donut, and line charts from carbon data, customized to your business needs and reporting requirements.
  • Support for Multiple Dimensions: Analyze data by GHG category, GHG scope, fiscal year, and more, with carbon emissions as the primary measure. 
  • Smart Context Handling: Joule understands follow-up questions, so you can keep exploring your data without starting over

For more information, see SAP Green Ledger, carbon emission analysis in SAP Discovery Center.

 

3.6. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: AI-assisted trend analysis and summary for reports (beta)

The Trend Analysis feature in Joule provides natural language interaction to help you analyze VAT returns. You can ask Joule to generate comparative trend reports and detect outliers. Joule delivers insights that make VAT reporting faster, more accurate, and easier to understand.

Joule can also summarize the generated reports, helping you review key trends and results quickly. This makes it easier to identify anomalies and support faster approvals.

Benefits:

  • Accelerates VAT review and approval cycles: Natural-language trend analysis and auto-summarized insights reduce the time required to interpret VAT data across periods, enabling faster reviews, quicker approvals, and shorter reporting cycles.
  • Improves accuracy and reduces compliance risk: Automated detection of trends, fluctuations, and outliers helps identify anomalies early, minimizing manual errors and lowering the risk of incorrect filings or regulatory non-compliance.
  • Enables informed, data-driven decision-making: Clear, comparable trend reports and concise summaries transform complex VAT data into actionable insights, helping finance and compliance teams make confident decisions with less effort.

Key features:

  • Use Joule to generate a trend analysis for a VAT return across multiple reporting periods. The analysis highlights key trends, fluctuations, and irregularities, and flags values that deviate from expected norms to help you identify potential errors or compliance risks.
  • Joule generates a concise summary of key VAT insights to support faster reviews, approvals, and decision-making.

 

3.7. SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, Cloud Edition: intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

Investigations are the central entities to examine security incidents in SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition. Intelligent recommendations automatically add case-specific response and mitigation recommendations to investigations based on predefined value lists and machine learning functionality. 

Benefits:

  • Decrease manual effort and increase efficiency for SAP monitoring agents

Key features:

  • Automated Recommendations: The system uses predefined value lists and machine learning to generate tailored recommendations for each investigation.
  • Guided Investigation Preparation: SAP monitoring agents are supported with automatic recommendations when compiling investigation results and suggestions for customers.
  • Standardized Response Generation: Recommendations are based on standard operating procedures for the detected alert patterns, ensuring consistency and reliability in incident response

For more information, see: Generating Investigation Recommendations

 

Sales

4.1. Automatic creation of sales orders from unstructured data (premium)

Internal sales representatives can create sales orders from purchase order files in PDF or image formats (unstructured data). After a file is uploaded, the system automatically extracts file information into a sales order request and proposes values for sales order request fields (for example, by determining the sold-to party based on extracted data). The sales order request can later be converted into a sales order.

Benefits:

  • Increase sales force efficiency through the automatic creation of sales orders from unstructured data
  • Reduce manual effort

Key features:

  • Automatic Data Extraction: Instantly extract and populate sales order details from unstructured files like PDFs and images
  • Intelligent Field Suggestions: The system proposes values for sales order fields, such as the sold-to party, based on the extracted data.
  • Multilanguage Support: SAP Document AI enables extraction and training in multiple languages, making the solution accessible for global teams.

For more information, see: Create sales orders from unstructured data

 

Procurement

5.1. Conversational search for local and central purchase requisition status (Release 2510)

Designed for casual users, who easily and quickly want to inform themselves about the status of their purchase requisition, the intelligent Joule assistant can be queried to fetch the follow-on document status of a requestor’s self-service purchase requisition

Benefits:

  • Provide quicker and easier access to the purchase requisition and follow-on document status

Key features:

  • Query Joule for the approval and follow-on document status of a given local or central purchase requisition without the need to navigate into the details of another SAP Fiori app
  • Enter any of the related document numbers—for example, local or central requisition number, purchase requisition number in the connected system, or cart number in SAP Ariba—when asking for the document status

 

Asset Management

6.1. Maintenance notification creation and screening with Joule (release 2602)

SAP Cloud ERP 2602 makes asset management easier for everyone.

Now, you can quickly create maintenance notifications just by telling Joule—using simple, everyday language—what the issue is, which equipment it affects, and how urgent it is. All your notifications are managed in the new SAP Fiori app, where you can easily follow up and turn them into maintenance orders with just a few clicks. This update makes reporting problems faster, reduces downtime, and takes the hassle out of routine maintenance tasks.

Benefits:

  • Simplified Maintenance Processes: Create maintenance notifications quickly and easily using natural language, reducing the complexity of traditional workflows
  • Faster Fault and Incident Response: Accelerate the reporting and handling of maintenance issues, enabling quicker resolution and less downtime.
  • Increased Efficiency: Streamline operations and boost productivity by leveraging AI-driven automation for routine asset management tasks.

 

Key features:

  • AI-Assisted Notification Creation: Use Joule to generate maintenance notifications by simply providing key details (description, technical object, priority) in natural language.
  • Integration with SAP Fiori App: Access and manage created notifications directly in the new SAP Fiori app “Manage Maintenance Notification (F5777)”
  • Conversational Order Creation: Accept requests and convert notifications into maintenance orders within a single conversational context.
  • Natural Language Processing: Perform operations and interact with the system using everyday language, making the process faster and more accessible.

For more information, see: What’s new for Joule – SAP Help Portal

 

Manufacturing

7.1. AI-assisted production routing proposal and copy routing (release 2602)

Save time and reduce errors by letting AI do the heavy lifting! Now, you can quickly generate new production routings by reusing existing ones—no more repetitive data entry.

Benefits:

  • Streamlined Process: Production engineers, planners, and master data specialists can easily create and edit routings, ensuring consistency and accuracy every time.

Key features: 

  • Use AI to find similar routing based on material name, description, and bill of material to propose close matches
  • Get recommendations of routing that you can copy or reuse, based on similarity
  • Copy and edit the proposed routing to arrive at the new routing

​As a prerequisite, you need to enable the related ISLM scenario. For information on setting up the ISLM scenario, see this blog post. For general information on ISLM, see: Intelligent Scenario Management.

For more information, see:

  • Intelligent Scenario to Find Similar Materials for Routing Proposal
  • Create a Routing using the AI-Assisted Material Search

 

7.2. Manage production orders - Production Planning and Operations agent (beta)

With this feature, you can start the Production Planning and Operations agent from the worklist of the Manage Production Orders app. The app now includes the Joule Release button that enables you to release your production orders assisted by agentic AI technology.

Benefits:

  • Reduce manual work with automated validation of material, capacity, and scheduling availability
  • Keep production moving with recommended workarounds for material and scheduling conflicts
  • Boost production throughput by cutting order-processing delays and minimizing downtime between order releases

Key features:

  • Assisting the production planners and supervisors in releasing up to 20 production orders at a time.
  • The agent automates prerequisite checks necessary for releasing production orders, including component availability and alternatives as well as work center capacity checks.
  • The agent also proposes rescheduling when the prerequisites for release are not met.
  • Once all criteria are met, the planner can confirm the proposed order changes, and the agent will complete the production order release.

For more information, see: Manage Production Orders - Production Planning and Operations Agent

Discovery Center

 

Joule For Consultants

 

Clearer Source Citations
SAP Joule for Consultants now makes it easier to see where information comes from. Citations are more visible and, when relevant, include results from public web searches. This gives you a transparent view of the sources behind every answer, helping you trust and verify the information you receive.

 

Expanded Reference Data
The SAP Enterprise Architecture Reference Library (EARL) is now partially available in Joule conversations. As more EARL content is added, you’ll see even richer and more accurate answers, with relevant references included right in your responses.

 

SAP Joule for Consultants Console

The SAP Joule for Consultants Console now offers new tools for both standard users and administrators. You can access the latest SAP release notes, usage statistics (for admins), a built-in Prompt Library, and system settings—all in one place

 

Access to Knowledge Base Articles and SAP Notes
Joule for Consultants now includes Knowledge Based Articles (KBA) and SAP Notes. If your company’s service agreement covers these, you’ll have access automatically—no extra steps needed for end-users. Administrators just need to make sure the right email addresses are assigned.

 

 

New Private Content

  • Simplification List: Stay up to date with changes in SAP S/4HANA. The Simplification List shows which features have changed, been removed, or replaced, so you can plan and adapt smoothly.
  • IMG (Implementation Guide): Easily find documentation and step-by-step activities to configure SAP functions for your organization, using a clear, structured navigation.

Quick Links for Faster Access
The “Show Prompt Guide” button is now “Quick Links.” This gives you instant access to the Console, Prompt Guide, and Prompt Library—all with a single click.

 

Longer Conversations Supported
You can now enter up to 10,000 characters in a single conversation—five times more than before. This is especially helpful for sharing longer code snippets or detailed questions.

 

Richer, Refreshed SAP Public Content
Joule for Consultants has updated and expanded its public SAP content. You’ll now find more detailed information and a wider range of insights from SAP Learning, SAP Help, SAP News, the AI Feature Catalogue, and more

 

For more information, see: What’s New for SAP Joule for Consultants

Discovery Center

 

The Future is Intelligent

The 2602 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud demonstrates our commitment to building an ERP that is not just a system of record, but a true partner in your success. By embedding AI across the suite, we are helping you unlock new levels of productivity, enhance decision-making, and create a more intuitive and enjoyable user experience for everyone.

This is just the beginning, and I can't wait to see how you leverage these new capabilities to drive your business forward.

 

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Artificial Intelligence as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the Cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

 

In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP Cloud ERP 2602 in the area of Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Enhanced integration of project plan information with SAP Cloud ERP (6JN) 
  • Project staffing view for resource managers (6JO) 
  • Allowing contingent workers (cost center procured) to record time on enterprise projects (1NT, 1Q4) 
  • Automatic removal of billing block for sales order billing plans (1NT) 
  • Joule as project assistant (beta), planned to be available mid-March
  • Further innovations

 

Check out my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

In today's fast-paced business environment, running projects efficiently, on time, and on budget is more critical than ever. This release is packed with features designed to bring more intelligence, automation, and transparency to your projects, ultimately driving significant business value. We’ve focused on streamlining processes, enhancing integration, and even giving you a glimpse into the future with AI-powered assistance.

Let's dive into the highlights that will help you unlock new levels of project success.

 

Enhancements in SAP Project and Resource Management

 

Enhanced integration of project plan information with SAP Cloud ERP

In complex projects spanning engineering, manufacturing, and services, a major challenge is keeping project execution plans in sync with core financial and logistical data. A delay in a design phase, for example, has a direct impact on procurement timelines and billing dates. If this information isn't shared instantly between the project planning tool and the core ERP, decisions are made based on outdated information. To solve this, our 2602 release supercharges the integration between SAP Project and Resource Management, our solution on the SAP Business Technology Platform, and SAP Cloud ERP. Now, when a project manager adjusts a project plan – such as extending a phase – the system automatically synchronizes these date changes with the core ERP in real time.

This direct synchronization ensures that your financial, commercial, and logistical data within SAP Cloud ERP always reflects the most current project reality, empowering you to make reliable decisions based on a single source of truth. By automating the update process, you eliminate the need for project teams to manually replicate date changes across systems, which significantly reduces administrative effort and minimizes the risk of human error. This tight integration also lays the groundwork for faster billing cycles, as milestone completions in the project plan can now directly trigger commercial processes in the core system.

 

Figure 1: Improved integration of project plan information between SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Project and Resource Management, enables better decision-making in projects through reliable, up-to-date project plan information.

 

Project staffing view for resource managers

Resource managers are constantly juggling demands from multiple projects, and gaining a clear, consolidated overview of the staffing situation for a specific project can be a fragmented and time-consuming process. To address this pain point, we are introducing a brand-new SAP Fiori app called “Manage Request Assignments”. This powerful application is designed specifically for resource managers, providing a single, project-centric view of all resource requests. It shows at a glance which roles have been fully staffed, which are partially staffed, and which are still open, eliminating the need to dig through multiple screens and reports.

This new application provides resource managers with unprecedented transparency into the staffing health of any project, allowing them to instantly identify bottlenecks and open demands without extensive research. Having all the necessary information in one place, with direct navigation to take action, streamlines the workflow and dramatically reduces the time it takes to staff open requests and get the right people on the right tasks. Ultimately, a clear, holistic overview empowers resource managers to more effectively optimize staffing within a single project or re-allocate experts across multiple projects, ensuring your most valuable resources are deployed with maximum impact.

 

Figure 2: The “Manage Request Assignments” application allows users to efficiently view the resource request and assignment details at a project level.

 

Other Innovations in SAP Project and Resource Management

Additional enhancements introduced in SAP Project and Resource Management. For example:

  • Technical user flow coverage (client credential flow) for some APIs
  • Use of actual dates in scheduling
  • Consideration of working and none working days in scheduling
  • Ability to block certain file types during file upload
  • Additional details for tasks and collaboration project

For a complete list and more details please take a look into the What's new document for SAP Project and Resource Management

 

 

Enhancements in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

 

Allowing contingent workers (cost center procured) to record time on enterprise projects

Modern project teams are a dynamic blend of internal employees and external contingent workers. A significant process gap existed for contingent workers who were procured via a cost center, as they couldn't use the standard “My Timesheet” application to record time against enterprise projects. This made it difficult to get a complete and timely picture of all labor hours and costs. With the 2602 release, we have closed this gap. Now, these contingent workers can use the very same “My Timesheet” application as internal employees to record their time against the projects they are staffed on, creating a unified experience for the entire team.

This enhancement delivers a unified and simplified process where all project resources, regardless of their employment type, use a single application for time recording, which improves the user experience. You can now capture every hour worked against your project demands in one central place, providing a more accurate and timely view of total project costs and progress. Furthermore, the system automates the creation of the corresponding service entry sheet and goods receipt upon timesheet approval, significantly reducing administrative overhead and ensuring your suppliers are paid accurately and on time.

 

Figure 3: Project-based and cost center-based contingent workers can record time seamlessly in the “Manage My Timesheet” application, ensuring consistent tracking and better visibility across all engagement types.

 

Automatic removal of billing block for sales order billing plans

Milestone billing is a fantastic way to align payments with project progress, but it has often been hampered by a manual step. Once a milestone was achieved, someone had to go into the sales order and manually remove the billing block to make that item available for invoicing, a delay that directly impacts cash flow. We have now fully automated this process. Building on a feature from our last release, the system now automatically removes the billing block from the corresponding billing plan item in the related sales order the moment a project manager confirms the completion of that milestone in the project.

By completely automating this process, you eliminate the manual hand-off and inherent delays, allowing you to invoice your customers the very moment a milestone is contractually met. This directly accelerates your order-to-cash cycle and improves cash flow. This automation also removes a tedious and error-prone manual task, freeing up your project controllers and sales administrators to focus their expertise on more strategic, value-added activities. The risk of human error is entirely eliminated, leading to a smoother, more reliable, and more accurate billing process from end to end.

 

Figure 4: For sales order billing plan items which are created based on enterprise project or WBS element milestones, the billing block is automatically removed when the related milestone is completed.

 

Joule as project assistant (beta), planned to be available mid-March

Setting up a new project can be a complex and time-consuming task, often requiring project managers to navigate between three or four different applications to define the scope, staff resources, and release the project for time recording. We are incredibly excited to give you a preview of how we are solving this with the future of project management: the Joule Project Assistant. This is our generative AI copilot, and we are starting with a beta agent designed to radically simplify the creation of internal projects. Instead of clicking through multiple screens, a project manager can simply ask Joule in natural language, e.g. "Create a new pre-sales project for the 'Rising Star' initiative, starting next week and lasting for one month.". Joule can then propose a template, adjust dates, staff the project, and release it – all within a single, conversational session.

This AI-powered assistant promises dramatic efficiency gains, condensing a multi-application process into a single, swift conversation that saves significant time and effort for your project managers. The intuitive, conversational nature of Joule makes project creation accessible to everyone, drastically reducing the learning curve and empowering users to initiate projects quickly and correctly. By leveraging templates from successful past projects, the Joule agent also helps ensure that new projects are set up consistently and in compliance with your company's standards right from the start. We are currently running a beta program for this and encourage you to get involved! Register for the Customer Engagement Initiative via this landing page as of March 15, 2026.

 

Figure 5: The AI-powered solution assists users in rapidly setting up projects by leveraging similar past projects when the volume is high, and projects are repetitive in nature.



Other innovations in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

We know how critical it is to have a clear, unified view of project financials. That's why we’ve introduced the company code currency into the Project Cost report. Now, you can see a consolidated view of all your key cost figures including planned costs, actuals, commitments, and even project stock costs – all in a single, consistent currency. This provides a much clearer picture of your project's overall financial health and variance.

There is one important prerequisite to keep in mind: for the report to display in the company code currency, all the projects and WBS elements you select for that report must share the same company code. This ensures data integrity and accurate reporting. For a more detailed guide on this feature, I encourage you to check out the documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

Second, we’ve Improved system usability by segregating the profitability segment from settlement rules for clearer allocation and analysis. What does this mean for you? In the past, assigning profitability might have required you to set up a settlement rule. Now, for projects with revenue, you can assign the profitability segment directly to a billing WBS element using the "Assign" function. This is a much more direct and intuitive workflow. It saves you steps, simplifies the allocation process, and makes your analysis clearer and more efficient. As with all our features, you can find comprehensive details on this enhancement in the SAP Help Portal.

Finally, and this is something I’m personally very excited about, we are going beyond system features to build a stronger community. We are officially introducing the new CIP community – a collaborative space designed for our customers, partners, and SAP experts to connect. The goal is to build a powerful network where we can all share ideas, discuss best practices, and co-innovate on the future of enterprise and collaborative project scenarios. This is your platform to influence the direction of our products and learn from your peers.

If you are interested in joining this community and helping shape the future of project management at SAP, please reach out to my colleagues, Anke Junker anke.junker@sap.com or Varsha Chetan Manu varsha.chetan.manu@sap.com, to get involved.


The SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release for EPPM is all about making your project management more connected, automated, and intelligent. From ensuring a single source of truth to simplifying daily tasks for every project role, these innovations are designed to deliver tangible business value and set you up for success.

Thank you for your time, and I look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities.

 

 

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

 

🔎Find all our assets

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Introducing the SAP Analytics Cloud Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit
entry.content:
Focus of this blog:

  • Introduction
  • How the UI Kit is set up
  • How to get the UI Kit
  • How to use the UI Kit
  • Essential Ressources

 

Introduction

The new SAP Analytics Cloud Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit is a high-fidelity Figma kit specifically designed for creating dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud Stories. It closely reflects Stories UI elements and is based on the SAP Fiori Morning Horizon theme.

The UI Kit includes ready-to-use dashboard templates with placeholder or example data, charts, and other UI controls. Designers can easily switch between templates, swap visualization types, and adapt dashboards as requirements evolve. This accelerates the design process while supporting a seamless transition from mock-ups to implementation and ensuring consistent results across projects.

👉 Download the UI Kit from GitHub

 

 

 

Why Use the SAC Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit?

  • Efficiency: Reduce repetitive design efforts with pre-built components, saving time and resources.
  • High-Fidelity Mockups: Design detailed and realistic dashboards that closely reflect the SAP Analytics Cloud capabilities.
  • Consistency: Adhere to established design standards to produce a cohesive look across various dashboards, enhancing overall user experience.

 

 

How the UI Kit is set up

The UI Kit consists of three pages for easy navigation and use.

The first page, “📓 Cover“, is a UI Kit overview that includes a cover, and a slide explaining the purpose and usage of the UI Kit, as well as semantic versioning.

"Cover" Page of the SAP Analytics Cloud Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit

The second page, “✏️ Analytical Pages”, provides dashboard templates. Depending on the purpose of your dashboard, you can choose to use different BI page types, or start with an empty page to design your dashboard from scratch.

"Analytical Pages" page of the SAP Analytics Cloud Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit

The third page, “✏️ Components”, contains all other assets: UI elements and controls, containers and different chart types available in SAP Analytics Cloud Stories.

"Components" page of the SAP Analytics Cloud Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit

 

How to get the UI Kit

  1. Navigate to the "SAC Fiori Horizon Theme" folder on GitHub.

  2. Open the file "SAC_Hi-Fi_Dashboard_UI_Kit.fig".
    Open the "SAC Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit" Figma File from GitHub
  3. Click on the Download button.
    Download the "SAC Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit" Figma File from GitHub

  4. Open the downloaded file in Figma. Choose a destination folder, and you're ready to go.

 

 

How to use the UI Kit

After importing the file, we suggest making the UI Kit available within your company's Figma space. This allows you to utilize Figma’s Library feature effectively. Depending on your settings, this setup enables either just your immediate team or your entire organization to access the UI Kit via the asset panel, fostering seamless collaboration and ensuring consistent design practices across your teams.
To share a UI Kit, head to the File Settings, and click on “Publish Library...”. Choose the components you wish to include and decide who should have access to the UI Kit. Then click on "Publish" to complete the process.


Note: This feature is not available if the file has been moved to your Drafts.

"Publish library..." menu option in Figma

You can now incorporate the SAP Analytics Cloud Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit into your files via the libraries dialog, making all components conveniently accessible from the assets panel.

If you prefer not to publish the UI Kit internally, you can easily copy and paste the components into your own design files or use them as local assets.

 

 

Are you ready to transform your dashboard design process?

Then download the SAC Dashboard Hi-Fi UI Kit from GitHub and get started today!

 

Essential Ressources

SAC Fiori Horizon Theme folder on GitHub

Dashboard Design Best-Practices

How to Design Low-Fidelity Dashboards with the SAP Analytics Cloud Dashboard Schematics UI Kit

entry.title: SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 2: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 and SAP Fiori Launchpad
entry.content:
This second post in my series gives an overview of user experience innovations that become generally available with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release. Highlights are further AI-assisted features: for personalizing My Home, getting explanations for error messages, and Situation Handling recommendations. We also provide the often-requested capability to launch apps via transaction code or SAP Fiori ID, the new “required reading” feature for news in My Home, and mobile phone optimization – to name a few.

Users in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can use Joule to access their data: via web browser on desktop, or mobile, via SAP Mobile Start, which also provides access to applications directly. Read about innovations for Joule and SAP Mobile Start in the first blog post introducing the series, and providing information about SAP Build Work Zone:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

Below, you will get an overview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition UX innovations in these areas:

SAP Fiori launchpad
  o.  New shell bar now the default
  o.  Launch apps directly via Transaction Code / Fiori-ID
  o.  Making New Apps Available to Users
      -  Activating new apps, since now delivered inactive
      -  New Experience Switch
   o.  Further improvements for the launchpad
My Home
  o.  Manage News app now with “must be read” feature
  o.  Enhanced Personalization
  o.  AI-Assisted UX Features:
       -  AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home for Applications
       -  AI-Assisted Error Explanation
       -  AI-Assisted Situation Handling
SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization
UI Adaptation
Improvements in details

Beyond these, we have a number of interesting new UX innovations for beta testing and early adopters with the 2602 release. I plan to cover these in my next blog post in the next few days.

Many of the UX improvements in details are enabled by our UI framework (SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements) and hence also available to customers and partners developing their own applications. Note however that the enabled AI-assisted features are only available for extensions built directly on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

Below you’ll see AI innovations from our UX framework as well as some selected application highlights. You can get a more comprehensive overview of the latest AI innovations in these blog posts, which also include application specific examples:

  • Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release.
  • AI innovations highlights in SAP Cloud ERP 2602.
  • Artificial Intelligence in SAP Cloud ERP 2602.

 

SAP Fiori Launchpad

Let’s look closer at these UX updates:

  • New shell bar now the default, with updated design of notifications and user menu.
  • Launch apps directly via Transaction Code / Fiori-ID.
  • Further improvements.

New shell bar now the default

We have made design improvements which are relevant for a consistent user experience across the entire SAP Business Suite. The new shell bar first became available as an opt-in choice for customers with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508 as described in my blog post covering 2508. With the 2602 release, it is now activated by default.

Benefits of the New Shell Bar

The new shell bar has these benefits:

  • The product name is displayed prominently now to the right of the SAP logo. In this case the name is “S/4HANA Cloud”.
  • Updated notifications design.
  • Updated user menu design.

Product Name Displayed

The immediately visible difference with the new shell bar is that the product name is displayed to the right of the SAP Logo, in this case “S/4HANA Cloud” as you can see at the top of Figure 1. The logo and the name are all contained within a clickable area, which brings the user to the start page (i.e. My Home).

Figure 1: New shell bar, now activated by default. Alt Text: A screenshot of Manage Sales Orders app, with the shell header highlighted, looking as described in the text.

Updated User Menu

The new user menu design shown in Figure 2 provides more space for the avatar of the user, and provides more space between items for easier selection. The settings are always at the top as the first entry, and the “Sign Out” option is separated from the actual navigation options in the list, to make it easier to find.

 

Figure 2: Updated design of user menu and notifications. Alt Text: On the left an image of the previous user menu and the updated user menu. On the right an image of the updated notifications list, showing four notifications in the group “Last Month”, each with a title, two lines of description and a line with the data and a “more” link.

Updated Notifications Design

The new simplified design of notifications shown on the right in Figure 2  fits better with the overall design of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and provides simplier interactions. By default notifications are sorted by date, with grouping by “Today”, “Yesterday”, “Last Week”, “Last Month”, as you can see in Figure 3 below. Users can also choose to sort by importance.

 Figure 3: Updated design of notifications. Alt Text: A short video showing the user selecting to display the list of notifications in the groups “Today”, “Yesterday” and “This Week”. 

Recommendation: Check Test Automation and RPA

We advise that you check any test automation and RPA (Robotic Process Automation) you may have, since you may need to make some adjustments – in particular if you access the user menu, for example to log off at the end of a test.

In case you need more time to do this, you can opt-out of the new shell bar for this release.You can switch it off via “Activate New Features”, Feature “/UI2/SHELL_BAR_TOGGLE”.

Important: do switch it back on before next upgrade! There is no opt-out planned from 2608 onwards.

Launch apps directly via Transaction Code

Customers have been requesting this for quite a while now, so I am happy to announce that with the 2602 release, users can launch applications directly via the transaction code (t-code).

This is particularly helpful for those users who have experience with older SAP ERP systems, such as SAP ECC (ERP Central Component), and are used to launching transactions by entering the “t-code” (transaction code) in the SAP GUI for Windows shell bar. To launch a transaction VA01 (Create sales order) in SAP GUI for Windows, users enter “/nVA01” if they want it to open in the same window, and “/oVA01” if they want it to open in a new window. Lower case “/nva01” or “/ova01” also works. Furthermore, users could go to the start page by entering “/n” or “/o”, opening it either in the same window or in a new window respectively.

We have implemented the same logic in the search field in the launchpad shell bar, but with “/o” now creating a new browser tab rather than opening a new window.

We have also extended it, so that users can also enter the t-code after “/n” or “/o”. Figure 4 shows what it looks like, and Figure 5 shows a short video of it in action for an example using /nmm03 (Display Material) to open the classic UI and /nF217A (Customer – 360 View) to open a Fiori app.

 

Figure 4: Launching a classic UI via the transaction code “va01”. Alt Text: The shell header is shown, with the search field showing “All” and the search term /nva01 entered.

 

Figure 5: Launching applications directly via transaction code. Alt Text: A short video showing the user entering /nmm03 and /nF217A as described in the text.

You can determine an application’s t-code via the About entry in the launchpad user menu. We have also assigned transaction codes to Fiori apps, they are often identical to the Fiori ID, but not always.

Read more about it in this blog post:

  • Navigate SAP Fiori launchpad with Ease: Launch Apps via Transaction Codes.

Making New Apps Available to Users

New apps for business users can help them work more effectively, and get their work done more easily – but only if they actually use them! That means that they need to be activated and hence made available to users in the first place.

When one or more new applications are delivered that replace an existing application, users of the existing application need to be informed that a replacement is available to them. This is handled by the New Experience Switch. This is especially important if the existing application is about to be deprecated.

The next two sections cover these two topics in more detail: activating new apps, and the New Experience Switch.

Activating new apps, since now delivered inactive

With the 2602 release we no longer activate new apps automatically, so as not to disrupt existing users, as well as to ensure that users are not automatically granted authorizations that they should not have.

In order to activate an application, i.e. a Fiori app or a classic UI, the underlying IAM  (Identity and Application Management) apps of type “App Authorization Variant” need to be activated. These particular IAM apps were introduced a while ago in order to allow finer-grained authorizations for applications.

For example, the My Inbox app has a standard view and an expert view. You will only be able to access the expert view if the corresponding IAM app for the expert view has been activated by your administrator. If your administrator has only activated the IAM app for the standard view, then you will be able to access the My Inbox app, but only its standard view.

Since new apps provided by SAP are no longer activated automatically, administrators need to activate the underlying IAM apps of type “App Authorization Variant”. Only then will users be able to find the apps via the launchpad search or in the App Finder, see the corresponding tiles on pages in the launchpad, and assign them as favorites in My Home.

Find out more in the documentation:

  • Work with IAM Apps (App Authorization Variants)
  • How to Activate or Deactivate IAM Apps (App Authorization Variants)

New Experience Switch

The New Experience Switch informs users that a replacement of the application that they have opened is available to them, and offers a toggle in the shell bar to directly switch to the new application. You can see what this looks like in the top left image in Figure 6.

If the existing, old version of the app is planned to be deprecated and removed in a future release beyond the next one, then the information icon in the shell bar next to the New Version toggle is replaced by a warning icon, as shown in the top right image in Figure 6.

Finally, if the existing, old version will be removed with the upgrade to the next release, an alert is shown instead of a warning, as shown in the bottom image in Figure 6.

Figure 6: New Experience Switch. Clockwise from top left: Information, Warning and Alert pop-overs. Alt Text: Three screenshots showing the respective popovers, each with a button “Switch to New Version”. The first two include a carousel giving information about the benefits of the new version. The last one include in red “August 2025” as the month when the app is planned to be removed.

The three phases described above were introduced already with the 2508 release.

With the 2602 release, we also support cases where one application has multiple successors. Figure 7 shows an example.

Figure 7: New Experience Switch warning for an application with multiple successors. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the popover containing two cards, one for each of the specialized versions available as successors.

Also new with 2602 is the option for users to provide feedback when the open up the new version of an application (Figure 8). When they select this, a survey is opened with one page of questions about the new version of the app.

Figure 8: New Experience Switch welcome pop-over for new app, now with “Provide Feedback” button. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the pop-over, with a carousel giving information about the benefits of the new version.

To find out more, have a look at the documentation, or at the SAP note for the list of apps that currently have successors making use of the New Experience Switch:

  • Documentation: Switching to a New App Version
  • SAP Note 3545277 - New Experience Switch in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Overview of Apps

Further Improvements for the Launchpad

Enterprise Search Shows All Smart Business Tiles

Business users can now find all their Smart Business tiles (typically insight tiles with KPIs and/or mini charts) using enterprise search in the launchpad shellbar. Since users can define their own tiles via Smart Business, with specific filter values, this means that they can also find these personal tiles using search.

Did you know that enterprise search supports a number of search operators that you can use when searching for apps, or indeed when searching for business objects? Operators such as OR, *, - and “”.

You can also further filter search results, export them, share them or save the search as a tile. Find out more in the documentation:

  • Searching and Launching Apps or Business Objects.

Administrators can Upload and Download Spaces

In the Manage Launchpad Spaces app of the SAP Fiori launchpad, administrators can download existing spaces together with their assigned pages and upload them again to another system.

This enables you to transfer spaces and their assigned pages to other systems, for example, when you can't use transports, meaning that you can improve productivity and consistency across systems. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Downloading and Uploading Spaces

 

My Home

We continue to enhance My Home to make it even more popular with users. In this release, we have:

  • Manage News app now with “must be read” feature
  • Enhanced Personalization
  • AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home for applications
    (this is covered in the next section AI-Assisted UX Innovations)

We are also providing the feature AI-Assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home for beta-testing, which I will showcase in part 3 in this series of blog posts.

Manage News App now with “Must be Read” Feature

News articles containing essential company information can now be designated as required reading. All users must acknowledge that they have read and understood the content.

The main benefits of this feature are:

  • Strengthens accountability: When reading is mandatory, employees can be held accountable for understanding essential information. This practice helps ensure compliance with legal, safety, and policy-related requirements.
  • Ensures consistent knowledge across the organization: All employees receive the same information, minimizing knowledge gaps and miscommunication.
  • Enhances change management : During periods of transition—such as the introduction of new policies or process changes—required reading ensures that all employees understand the reasons behind and the impacts of those changes.

Figure 9 shows what this looks like for a user: a required reading pop-over appears on their screen. The article has also been flagged as “Containing Critical News”.

Figure 9: An example of a news article that is required reading. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home with a pop-over “Required Reading” that is also highlighted with “Contains Critical News”. The news article has an image at the top, followed by text showing the first part of the article. The “Done” button is greyed out, the “Remind me later” button is visible.

Figure 10 below shows the Manage News app, with the toggles Critical and the new Required Reading one.

Figure 10: The Manage News app has a toggle to mark articles as “Required Reading”. Alt Text: Screenshot of the Manage News app with the toggles set to “Yes”, and some news text in the Description field.

Enhanced Personalization

A great strength of My Home is the degree to which users can personalize it to suit their needs. We have now enhanced personalization further by letting users control the visibility of News and Pages separately within the News and Pages section of My Home.

If they are not so interested in news, they can choose to hide it, with the additional benefit of having all the pages only take up one row of vertical space rather than two, as shown in Figure 11. Figure 12shows how users can do the personalization.

Note that even with the news hidden, they will still receive required reading: when they log on, the required reading appears as a pop-over that can only be closed by confirming that one has read and understood the content.

Alternatively, users can decide to only show news and not pages. They can of course also decide to show both news and pages, as before, or hide both.

Figure 11: My Home with enhanced personalization, showing News deselected and Pages selected. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home, with eight cards in one row in the Pages section, below the ToDos section showing one situation and the Apps section.

Figure 12: My Home Settings pop-over with the new selectors for News and Pages within the News and Pages section. Alt Text: A screenshot showing the Layout part of My Home Settings, where users can select or deselect sections. For the News and Pages section, News is deselected, Pages are selected.

 

AI-Assisted User Experience

When AI is embedded directly into the application UI to give users a better user experience, as opposed to being accessed via the Joule window, we talk about “AI-Assisted User Experience”.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508, we distinguish between AI base features and premium features:

  • Base features are free of charge, and only require that the customer signs the SAP AI Terms, which can be done via this link.
  • Premium features require SAP AI Units to be in place. Those listed here also require Joule Premium for Financial Management. Customers can activate this via SAP for Me -> Portfolio & Products -> Business AI.

The following are now generally available as premium features (they were both in beta testing with the 2508 release) and are described in more detail below:

  • AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home for applications.
  • AI-assisted error explanation.

This feature is still in beta, but planned to become generally available with the 2602 release on March 12th:

  • AI-assisted situation handling.
    See below for more details.

AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home for Applications

You could already use natural language and AI to personalize your My Home by adding Insights Cards, since the 2508 release. Now, with 2602, you can use natural language to find and add applications and insights tiles.

To do this, use the AI button “Add Content” in the salutation bar at the top of My Home, as shown at the top of Figure X. Clicking on this brings a pop-over with the option to add Insights Cards or, as shown, Apps & Insights Tiles. By describing what you want to do, the system will propose suitable applications and Insights Tiles. If you select them and click “Add”, they will be added, either as an entry in your Apps Favorites section, or in the Insights Tiles section. Figure 13 shows these main steps.

Figure 13: AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home for Applications. Alt Text: The image shows three screenshots: at the top an extract of My Home showing the blue salutation bar, with an AI button “Add Content” on the right. An arrow has been added linking it to the second screenshot, where a popover “Add Content” is shown, where the user has entered “I want to monitor sales orders what app should I use to do this”, and two of four results are listed and visualized: “Track Sales Orders” and “Manage Sales Orders” (shown as an insight tile). Below that a screenshot of the outcome in My Home: the former is added to Apps Favorites, the latter to Insights Tiles.

 Watch a demo and read more:

  • Video: AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home for Applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (1:07 min.)
  • SAP Discovery Center: Smart Personalization of My Home.

AI-Assisted Error Explanation

New users who are unfamiliar with the system may find that the short error messages give too little information for them to fully understand the issue, and what to do about it. This is where the Summarizing and Guiding and Recommending AI-patterns can help: the AI-Assisted Error Explanation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition uses these to summarize information which explains the error and provide a recommendation for how to resolve it. Figure 14 shows an example where a user has entered a Sold-to-Party ID which does not exist in the system.

Note that this feature is also only available for applications based on SAP Fiori elements.

Figure 14: AI-Assisted Error Explanation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502. Alt Text: On the left part of a data entry screen is shown, with the field “Sold-to Party containing “ABC” marked red and below that a  popover with an error message “No customer master record exists for sold-to-party ABC”, and in the text line below that the AI icon followed by “Generate Explanation”. A fat arrow leads from this text to the image on the right, which indicates what happens when you click on this text: you get a popover with an short Explanation section and a longer Resolution section with numbered actions.

Have a look:

  • Video: AI-Assisted Error Explanation (45 seconds)
  • SAP Discovery Center: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, error explanation.

AI-Assisted Situation Handling

The Intelligent Situation Handling framework helps users deal with not-so-frequent business situations, by notifying them of the situation, giving them an overview of the situation and providing contextual information to help resolve it. Ideally, the system also offers recommendations on how to go about resolving it.

Currently still in beta testing, we plan to make AI-assisted situation handling generally available with the 2602.1 release on March 12th.

Figure 15 shows what this looks like for a situation where a purchase contract is going to expire soon. The system is able to evaluate the company policy, in this case provided in the form of a decision matrix, and informs the user that a Request for Proposals is not needed for this contract, since its contract value is below the threshold.

Figure 15: AI-Assisted Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, showing how the user is notified in My Home and can navigate to the situation page and there generate a recommended solution. ALT Text: The To-Do card “The purchase contract will expire soon” is shown at the top, with an arrow leading to the image below of the situation page, showing a “General Info” section and a “Recommended Solution” section, which only contains the text “You haven’t generated a solution yet. Would you like to do that now”, along with a “Generate” button with the AI icon. From there an arrow leads to the bottom image which shows the recommended solution text, along with thumbs-up and thumbs-down feedback buttons.

To see it in action, watch this:

  • Video: AI-Assisted Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (1:00 minutes).
  • SAP Discover Center: AI-assisted Situation Handling.

The extended framework for Situation Handling allows customers to define their own situations. It is very powerful, but we have received feedback that it can be challenging for first time customers and partners to get it up and running. Hence Angelika Salmen has published this blog post to help you get started:

  • My First Situation with the Extended Framework.

For further information about Situation Handling, have a look at the links at the end of the above blog post. 

 

SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization

We have made significant improvements in the overall user experience for accessing selected applications from your mobile phone, with layouts and interactions tailored specifically for smartphones.

The recommended way to access SAP Fiori web applications from your mobile phone is via SAP Mobile Start, which treats web applications the same way as native mobile applications: users can define favorites, and see all their apps, both web and native mobile, in the Apps view.

The currently supported SAP Fiori elements based applications are now rendered in a more user-friendly way, reducing complexity for users on the go by removing non-essential features, patterns and services that are not suited for mobile use.

This results in improved productivity and speed with simplified navigation, faster access to actions and optimized information density.

The new layout is provided by the mobile mode. Users can switch on the previous desktop mode in case they need access to any of the non-essential features hidden in the mobile mode. Figure 16 shows an example of a list report in the desktop mode (on the left) and what it now looks like rendered in the mobile mode (on the right).

Figure 16: SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization. On the left a list report rendered using the previous desktop mode, on the right using the new mobile mode. Alt Text: screenshots of a list report as it would appear on a mobile phone. On the left with filter bar and filters visible, so that only one line of the list is shown; on the right they are hidden, so that three lines of content are shown.

Currently these five apps are supported:

  • My Home
  • Customer 360 View (F2187A)
  • Manage Direct Activity Allocation (F3697A)
  • Manage Material Serial Numbers (F6879)
  • Manage Production Supply Areas (F6935)
  • Serial Number History (F7368)

We are eager to learn from our customers to see how well the current functionality meets users real-life needs, and where we could improve the experience. If you are interested in trying this out and providing feedback, please sign up for our Early Adopter Care program.

 

UI Adaptation

Adapt UI is a key user extensibility tool for making no-code / low-code, upgrade-safe adaptations and extensions to SAP Fiori apps. We have continuously enhanced its scope, so that it now supports most of the common changes.

A key user is a business expert who is authorized to make changes on behalf of other users – for example a team member who is interested in technology. The goal of key user extensibility is to empower business users to be able to adapt their working environment to suit their needs, without having to rely on system administrators.

Adapt UI is easy and safe to use: you can undo/redo changes, revert to a previously activated version or even revert to the original app.

Many Fiori apps contain object pages, that summarize information on a business object such as a sales order or a product. Adapt UI offers a large amount of options for adapting and extending object pages:

  • Hide/show SAP-defined fields
  • Add custom fields created with the SAP Fiori app Custom Fields (ID F1481)
  • Combine/split/rearrange/group/ungroup fields
  • Hide/show SAP-defined tabs, sections, tables, or action buttons
  • Change the configuration of tables (view settings, initial load, scrolling thresholds etc. – as featured in my previous blog post)
  • Change text arrangement (ID only, Text first, Text las) – also featured in my previous blog post.
  • Rename labels
  • Hide/show object header content
  • Embed web-based content in the object header or a section.

Key users who are authorized to use adapt UI can open it directly from their user menu in the launchpad. Once in started, Adapt UI shows you directly on the screen what changes you can make.

To find out more about this and watch a demo showing how it works and how easy it is, I thoroughly recommend this 13-minute video:

Video 1: Extending SAP Fiori Object Pages with Adapt UI. Click on the image to go to the video.The video shows you:

  • How to recognize an object page
  • What is Adapt UI
  • Prerequisites for Adapt UI
  • Demo
  • Tips for Working with Adapt UI
  • Key Takeaways
  • Resources worth knowing. These are:
    • SAP Design System - SAP Fiori: Object Page Floorplan
    • SAP Help - SAP Fiori Launchpad User Guide: ​Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime – Key User Adaptation.
    • SAP Learning - Tutorials: Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications.

Note that the video was created for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, so the transport step at the end is different in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – all the rest is relevant also for public edition, which is why I included it here.

The Adapt UI functionality is powered by SAPUI5 Flexibility technology.

 

Improvements in Details

User research has shown that it is easier to work with tables that contain hundreds of entries when the tables are implemented as a grid table rather than a responsive table.

Hence, with the 2602 release, we have looked at apps containing tables that typically have hundreds of entries and have changed the type of table used from a responsive table to a grid table.

For the full list of enhancements, have a look at the What’s New Viewer:

  • UX in What’s New Viewer for  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

 

Continue reading

I hope you enjoyed this second part, covering what is now generally available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 and SAP Fiori launchpad. Here's a link to part 3, covering exciting new UX innovations available for beta testing with the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 3: AI (Beta) Innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

entry.title: My Home in SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Good news – My Home is finally available with SAP BTP ABAP environment!

My Home is your personalized entry page that provides you with easy access to the business insights and applications you need to do your job with greater speed and agility. My Home for SAP BTP ABAP Environment lets you access and manage your favorite pages, your favorite apps, and your insights on any device. It follows a modular approach to meet the needs of different roles and builds on the functionality of the SAP Fiori launchpad using spaces and pages.

 

New Look and Feel

My Home will be set as default for newly provisioned and existing systems as of SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2602. Accordingly, this soon changes the appearance of the SAP Fiori launchpad for every user. So, look forward to the new look and feel!

However, the setting to disable My Home will still be available for administrators until the release upgrade SAP BTP ABAP environment 2608. Then, My Home will be the one and only entry page within SAP BTP ABAP environment.

My Home in SAP BTP ABAP environment

 

 

My Home Sections

My Home is divided into different sections. You can view your News, Pages, Apps, Insights Tiles, and Insights Cards on this page.

 

News

The News section keeps you updated on your company's latest news, or SAP BTP ABAP environment system news.

News section on My Home entry page

 

Pages

The Pages section provides you with quick access to your favorite or most used pages. You can manage the visibility of your pages and order them in a way that enhances your productivity.
